reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only — they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades — never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

down_bias_gate.note: restrict-only: harvest DOWN/TV asymmetry by blocking proven-losing UP stacks

mtf_gate.note: observe-only conflict veto: blocks 4m/5m fast-pair conflict only (loop arch default). require_confirm/side_align are opt-in restrict-only layers, not trade authority.

confidence_tier.note: At 15m TTC sweet spot, TV MTF regime adjusts min_edge/max_price only. TV trade gates remain off per operator lock.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade — learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall WR 60.4%, +1.35 USD) driven by a handful of high-n buckets that clear the n>=8 filter and exceed breakeven after costs; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, dead volume, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD and strong-trend ADX are the clearest confirmed edges. Most other slices remain below reliable sample size or show flat-to-negative realized PnL, indicating the current selectivity is still largely noise-filtered rather than broadly profitable. No material change from prior (first analysis).
working: bearish HTF / bearish supertrend / strong ADX (n=17-31, WR 0.645-0.655)
failing: bullish HTF bias + bullish supertrend (n=24-36, WR ~0.54-0.58, negative PnL)
warnings: 53 trades total; many promising buckets still <30 samples and could revert
all data from observe-only mode; live slippage/queue priority unknown
focus_next: accumulate samples inside the confirmed bearish/trending/120-240s/normal-BB cells
track whether DOWN_STRONG edge persists above 200 total trades
monitor any degradation in the 0.73 WR TTC bucket as n grows

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (Hurst), normal BB state, dead volume, strong ADX trend, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, z-score -2..1, and 120-240s TTC show confirmed positive EV (win rates 0.64-0.83, n>=12-50, all reconciled). UP signals, long holds (>=240s), active volume, range-top/breakouts, and mixed MTF remain negative or marginal. Sample still small overall; patterns stable vs noise but require more data.

With 53 settled trades the composite signal shows modest positive EV (overall WR 0.604, +1.35 USD) driven by a handful of high-sample regimes; DOWN/trending/strong-trend/normal-BB/sell-pressure setups are the only buckets >=8 samples that are clearly profitable after costs. Most other slices remain too small or mixed to confirm. No material change from prior zero-sample state beyond accumulation of these 53 observations.
changes_since_last: First analysis; sample grown from 0 to 53 settled trades

history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed positive EV (win rate 0.69, avg_ev 0.136) while overall sample remains too small for broad generalization. Strongest confirmed patterns are bearish_aligned MTF, sell_pressure CVD, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC and strong_trend ADX; UP signals and >=240s TTC remain marginal or negative. No material changes possible without prior_analysis; all n<8 buckets ignored.

With n=53 total settled trades, overall WR 60.4% and positive avg_ev 0.116 after costs, but only a handful of buckets meet n>=8 with WR confidently above breakeven and stable positive EV. Confirmed edges appear in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, and DOWN_STRONG signals; most other slices remain too sparse or mixed to trust.

With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.69, ev 0.136) while overall sample remains too small for most buckets; normal BB state, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, and 120-240s TTC stand out as profitable with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. UP signals and >=240s TTC remain unprofitable despite positive avg_ev. No prior analysis available so all patterns treated as initial observations.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, bearish MTF alignment, and CVD sell pressure show confirmed positive EV (win rates 0.64-0.83, n>=12-50, avg_ev>0.11). Patterns like >=240s TTC, active volume, and mixed MTF alignment are failing with negative realized PnL despite some positive avg_ev. Sample remains small overall; no material changes possible without prior analysis.

With 53 settled trades, overall win rate 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (+0.116) but tiny total PnL (+1.35 USD); only buckets n>=8 are considered. Confirmed edge in trending/Hurst, z-score -2..1, TTC 120-240s, BB normal, ADX strong trend, CVD sell pressure, bearish MTF alignment, and DOWN direction. Most other slices remain too small or show negative realized PnL despite decent win rates.

With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall WR 60.4%, +1.35 USD) driven by a handful of high-n buckets that clear the n>=8 filter and exceed breakeven after costs; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, dead volume, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD and strong-trend ADX are the clearest confirmed edges. Most other slices remain below reliable sample size or show flat-to-negative realized PnL, indicating the current selectivity is still largely noise-filtered rather than broadly profitable. No material change from prior (first analysis).

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

headlines: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.79B net outflows last week (IBIT $1.30B)
Bitcoin not near bottom yet, could drop another 50%+

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

last_note.summary: 136 settled trades, 63.2% win rate, +$51.81 realized PnL (1.22 profit factor). DOWN side heavily dominates (116 trades, 65.5% WR) vs UP (20 trades, 50% WR). Edge model severely miscalibrated: 0.3-0.4 bucket predicts UP but realizes only 29.4% empirical, 0.2-0.3 predicts DOWN but realizes 52.6% UP. 230 of 237 execution rejections are 'underdog_price_below_floor'—a hard filter blocking most UP trades and creating artificial DOWN bias.

knob_recommendations.reason: 230/237 rejections from this filter; blocking valid UP trades and causing severe directional imbalance
Current avg_ev_after_costs is 0.103; tighten threshold to reduce model calibration error in 0.3-0.4 bucket
Keep flat $5 sizing until edge model recalibration fixes directional prediction errors
538 down_bias_gate rejections suggest gate is too aggressive; relax slightly to test UP side in balanced sample

new_lessons.rule: underdog_price_below_floor rejection (230/237) blocks UP trades disproportionately; must lower threshold or remove to test true edge
0.3-0.4 bucket predicts UP but realizes 29.4% UP; 0.2-0.3 predicts DOWN but realizes 52.6% UP—model is directionally inverted
DOWN: 116 trades, 65.5% WR is sample-backed edge; UP: 20 trades, 50% WR insufficient sample due to execution filter bias
Brier 0.239 and log_loss 0.676 acceptable but driven by DOWN majority; UP prediction quality unknown due to n=20
Avg loss $4.70 > avg win $3.34; profit factor 1.22 < 1.5 gate—need tighter stop-loss or higher confidence threshold

research_loop.note: strategy-layer research meta-loop: independent model proposes exploit/avoid contexts + bounded knob nudges + lessons. Observe-only unless auto_apply (whitelisted+bounded). PAPER ONLY.

recent.rule: 97% of execution rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; current floor filters low-edge trades but rejects too many—nudge up 3-4 cents
only 4 context buckets populated, all n=1; cannot identify exploit/avoid contexts until 30+ samples per bucket—expand feature coverage or relax gates
if avg_loss > avg_win, profit_factor will remain <1.5 even at 65% WR; enforce max_loss < 0.85*avg_win
116 down vs 20 up indicates feature/gate imbalance; audit down_bias_gate and directional_allowlist to restore 40/60 up/down mix
230 underdog_price_below_floor rejections is 61% of gate candidates—relaxing floor incrementally (0.38→0.35) will test if sub-0.40 dogs have hidden edge
brier 0.239 vs baseline 0.25 is negligible improvement; recalibrate or retrain edge model with larger labeled set (136 samples insufficient for 6 buckets)
n=1 in all pnl_by_* breakdowns means zero statistical power; require min 20 samples per bucket before any exploit/avoid decision
underdog_price_below_floor rejection (230/237) blocks UP trades disproportionately; must lower threshold or remove to test true edge

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop + a liveness watchdog (last_beat_age_s/stalled). PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

last_violations.description: 15m up-mid below nested 5m up-mid: P(up_15m) cannot be < P(up_5m) for overlapping window

dependency_arbitrage.note: LCMM nested-window scanner + optional paper execution on validated nested_implication violations.

arb_graph.note: Graph is rebuilt each tick from open windows; Grok edges are never tradable alone.

proposal.description: Both 5m windows are strictly contained inside the 15m window 642867 (11:15-11:30 ET).

baseline_cohort_gate.note: baseline quant path: 180-240s TTC band (scaled on 15m), high edge + strong CEX; UP blocked until promoted; green_path=15m DOWN cohort only (TV observe-only)

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective — never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

bucket_evidence.rule: blocked iff confidently_losing AND fdr_significant (Wilson upper < breakeven, PF < min, BH-FDR q=0.10)

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.

arbitrage.note: arb halt only on anomaly (guaranteed_profit<=0); risk-free P&L is segregated

dependency_arbitrage.note: Lane B halt on booking anomaly only; walk-forward is observability

stop_conditions.note: quantitative kill switches checked each tick from settled ledger evidence; NOT the LLM's opinion. Halting blocks NEW entries only.

execution_realistic_edge.note: KL divergence is observe-only — large KL means model disagrees with market; never a buy signal when model Brier > market Brier.

arbitrage.note: risk-free dutch book: BUY-both (asks<$1) + SELL-both (mint $1 set, sell bids>$1); deterministic P&L; NEVER blended into directional. PAPER ONLY.

With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.69, ev 0.136) while overall sample remains too small for most buckets; normal BB state, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, and 120-240s TTC stand out as profitable with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. UP signals and >=240s TTC remain unprofitable despite positive avg_ev. No prior analysis available so all patterns treated as initial observations.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, bearish MTF alignment, and CVD sell pressure show confirmed positive EV (win rates 0.64-0.83, n>=12-50, avg_ev>0.11). Patterns like >=240s TTC, active volume, and mixed MTF alignment are failing with negative realized PnL despite some positive avg_ev. Sample remains small overall; no material changes possible without prior analysis.

With 53 settled trades, overall win rate 60.4% and positive avg EV after cost (+0.116) but tiny total PnL (+1.35 USD); only buckets n>=8 are considered. Confirmed edge in trending/Hurst, z-score -2..1, TTC 120-240s, BB normal, ADX strong trend, CVD sell pressure, bearish MTF alignment, and DOWN direction. Most other slices remain too small or show negative realized PnL despite decent win rates.

With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall WR 60.4%, +1.35 USD) driven by a handful of high-n buckets that clear the n>=8 filter and exceed breakeven after costs; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, dead volume, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD and strong-trend ADX are the clearest confirmed edges. Most other slices remain below reliable sample size or show flat-to-negative realized PnL, indicating the current selectivity is still largely noise-filtered rather than broadly profitable. No material change from prior (first analysis).

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

cex_lead_edge.promotion_rule: n>=min AND wilson_lower(win_rate)>breakeven AND Brier_cex<Brier_market AND avg_pnl>0
cex_lead_edge.note: CEX-lead latency edge; benchmark is the MARKET price (already ~0.21 Brier). SHADOW grades only; GATED can PROPOSE a side on a proven bucket but the execution-quality gate + safety floor remain authoritative. PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

trading_performance.note: Profitability, win-rate, side balance (DOWN>UP), and ledger integrity.

operation.note: Engine health: reconciliation, loops, stops, and pipeline activity.

external_signals.note: External signal quality and how well signals align with bot outcomes.
